{{Quote|It's got a trace on it. Coming from... the Senate. Hang on.. Hmmm. Do you know a Senator Sano Sauro? It looks like he's interested in what Tyro Caladian is up to.|Alam Syk, to Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="TSF" />}}
'''Alam Syk''' was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] serving the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="TSF" /> In [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Syk had come to be known as one of the foremost security experts at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. During that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Syk was contacted by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] regarding the alleged kidnapping of [[Gillam Tarturi]], the son of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Berm Tarturi]]. Reviewing the security holos from the evening of the crime, neither Kenobi or Syk had any luck finding anything out of the ordinary with the seemingly inconclusive tapes. Later, Kenobi asked Syk to trace the [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] signal of his friend, the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] [[Aide/Legends|aide]] [[Tyro Caladian]], back to its source, leading the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to Senator [[Sano Sauro]], the man who had bugged Caladian's communication equipment.<ref name="TSF" />

==Behind the scenes==
Alam Syk's voice appeared in [[Jude Watson]]'s [[2003]] novel ''[[Jedi Quest: The School of Fear]]''.<ref name="TSF" />

*''[[Jedi Quest: The School of Fear]]'' {{1st}} {{Voice}}
